# `*.component.ron` — field reference

<!-- GENERATED by `cargo run -p dc_data_docs` (dronecom #2815). Do not edit: field docs live as /// comments on the Rust structs. -->

Data: [`definitions/components/`](../definitions/components/)

## `ComponentDefinition`

Top-level component definition loaded from a `.component.ron` asset file.

Each file defines a discrete named equipment item with fixed stats.

### `id` : [`ComponentId`](#componentid)

Stable slug identifying this component (e.g. `"sps_49"`). Referenced by
blueprints and save files; distinct from the display `designator`.

### `designator` : [`Designator`](#designator)

Military-style designation shown in the UI (e.g. `"SPS-49"`). Combined
with `codename` by `Self::full_name` into the full name.

### `codename` : [`Codename`](#codename)

Codename shown in the UI (e.g. `"Long Range Radar"`) — the second half of
the full display name.

### `category` : [`ComponentCategory`](#componentcategory)

Which `ComponentCategory` this item belongs to (`Sensors` /
`Warheads` / `Utility` / `CarriedEntities`) — drives
mount-compatibility filtering.

### `mount_rule` : [`MountRule`](#mountrule)

How many of this component may be mounted on one vehicle (#1644).

### `weight` : f32

Component weight in kg — counts against the chassis's `max_weight` budget.

### `power_draw` : f32

Steady-state electrical power draw in kW, summed with the chassis base
draw and other components into the vehicle's total power demand.

### `allowed` : `Vec`<[`ChassisFilter`](#chassisfilter)>

Chassis-compatibility whitelist: this component may be mounted only on a
chassis matching at least one filter. Empty means any chassis is allowed.

### `payload` : [`ComponentPayload`](#componentpayload)

The type-specific payload (`ComponentPayload`) — sensor suite, warhead,
or utility — that gives the component its effect.

## `ComponentId`

Identifier for a component definition, used as a registry lookup key.

### `0` : `String`

The component definition's registry key string.

## `Designator`

Military-style designator (e.g. "MQ-220", "TF-340").

### `0` : `Arc`<str>

The designator string (e.g. "MQ-220").

## `Codename`

Human-readable codename (e.g. "Lynx", "Gale").

### `0` : `Arc`<str>

The codename string (e.g. "Lynx").

## `ComponentCategory`

Category of a mountable component.

Variants:

- **`Sensors`** — Detection equipment (radar, sonar, RWR, IR, visual sensors).
- **`Warheads`** — Weapon payloads (warheads); at most one per vehicle.
- **`Utility`** — Support equipment with no sensor or warhead payload.
- **`CarriedEntities`** — Carried sub-vehicles / munitions — blueprints stowed aboard for launch.

## `MountRule`

How many of a component may be mounted on a single vehicle.

Applies to mounted `Component` inventory items only — carried blueprints
(munitions) and fuel are unaffected. Enforced by
`dc_blueprints::validate_recipe` over the top-level mounted components.

Variants:

- **`Many`** — Unlimited copies allowed (e.g. generic hardpoints / utility items).
- **`Single`** — At most one of *this* component (matched by `ComponentId`). Blocks identical duplicates; two *different* components of the same kind are still allowed.
- **`SoleInCategory`** — At most one, and it may not share its vehicle with any other component of the same `ComponentCategory` (e.g. only one warhead, of any type).

## `ChassisFilter`

Filter that matches chassis by domain or physics model.

Variants:

- **`Domain`**([`Domain`](#domain)) — Matches any chassis in this physical `Domain` (Air / Sea / Submarine).
- **`Physics`**([`PhysicsModel`](#physicsmodel)) — Matches any chassis using this `PhysicsModel`.

## `ComponentPayload`

Type-specific payload data carried by a component definition.

Variants:

- **`Sensor`**(`Vec`<[`SensorDef`](#sensordef)>) — A detection component: one or more sensors (`SensorDef`). Multiple entries let a single component mount a sensor suite.
- **`Warhead`**([`WarheadDef`](#warheaddef)) — A weapon payload: a single warhead (`WarheadDef`). At most one warhead per vehicle.
- **`Utility`** — A support component with no sensor or warhead payload (fuel tanks, datalinks, structural/hardpoint items); its effect is its weight, power draw, and category.

## `Domain`

Operational domain for a chassis or entity.

Variants:

- **`Air`** — Operates in the air — a fixed- or rotary-wing aircraft.
- **`Sea`** — Operates on the sea surface — a ship or surface craft.
- **`Submarine`** — Operates below the sea surface — a submarine.

## `PhysicsModel`

Physics model variant for a chassis.

Variants:

- **`FixedWing`** — Fixed-wing flight — lift-borne aircraft and cruise missiles.
- **`RotaryWing`** — Rotary-wing flight — helicopters, which can hover.
- **`SeaSurface`** — Surface vessel moving on the water plane.
- **`Submarine`** — Submerged vessel moving in three dimensions below the surface.

## `SensorDef`

Sensor component parameters.

### `kind` : [`SensorKind`](#sensorkind)

Detection modality (radar, active/passive sonar, RWR, IR, visual) —
selects the signal-propagation law and any clutter/notch model.

### `capability` : [`SensorCapability`](#sensorcapability)

Sensor role and track-file capacity (DC-946.1). Carries the DC-946.4 /
#540 `lock_detection` flag on its search-bearing variants.

### `threshold` : f32

Minimum received signal strength for detection. A contact registers once
its signature strength, attenuated by range, exceeds this floor; a lower
threshold is more sensitive and detects at longer range (see
`SignalFalloff::detectable`).

### `scan_interval` : f32

Seconds between sweeps.

### `acquire_dwell_secs` : f32

Fire-control acquisition dwell (s): how long a cued track must hold inside
the acquisition gate before the lock transitions `Acquiring → Locked`
(#2201). A platform search/FCS radar confirms a new track over ~1.0 s; a
fired weapon's terminal seeker is slaved to the launch solution and
acquires near-instantly (~0.2 s). `0.0` locks on the first satisfying scan.

### `bearing_rate_tau_secs` : f32

EMA time constant (s) for the seeker LOS-rate fed to bearing-only PN guidance (#1696). Shorter than the global published-bearing τ so terminal guidance stays responsive; only passive bearing tracks consume it.

### `is_active` : bool

Whether this is an active (emitting) sensor rather than a passive
listen-only one. An active sensor radiates only under EMCON Active
(silenced otherwise), and its `emits` / `emission_strength` take effect
only while active; a passive sensor always listens and cannot be
EMCON-silenced.

### `emits` : `Option`<[`SensorKind`](#sensorkind)>

When active & enabled, this sensor emits a signal detectable by sensors of this kind.

### `emission_strength` : f32

Signal strength of the emission.

### `fov_h` : f32

Horizontal field of view in degrees (0-360).

### `fov_v` : f32

Vertical field of view in degrees (0-180).

### `mounting` : [`MountingPosition`](#mountingposition)

Physical mounting position -- determines Y-offset for horizon calculations.

### `iff_threshold` : f32

IFF identification threshold -- signal strength above which a sensor
can identify a contact's affiliation. 0.0 means detection only (no IFF).

### `spreading_exponent` : f32

Spreading exponent for signal propagation. Determines the power law:
signal ~ 1/R^n for passive, 1/R^(2n) for active.
EM sensors use 2.0 (physics-fixed); sonar sensors use ~1.2 (ocean-dependent).

### `characteristics` : [`SensorCharacteristics`](#sensorcharacteristics)

Kind-specific detection characteristics (notching, clutter).

### `physics` : [`SensorPhysics`](#sensorphysics)

First-principles physical parameters (DC-1013). The runtime
measurement-noise + resolution are *derived* from these, not authored.

### `authored_noise` : [`AuthoredNoise`](#authorednoise)

Measurement sigmas with no first-principles formula (velocity, altitude,
FDOA) — kept authored (DC-1013).

### `signature_capability` : [`SignatureCapability`](#signaturecapability)

Signature-recognition capability (DC-36.6): micro-Doppler (radar) /
DEMON (sonar). `None` for basic search sensors.

### `elevation` : [`ElevationCapability`](#elevationcapability)

Elevation / height-finding capability (DC-1049). Orthogonal to `kind`
and `is_active`. `None` = 2D sensor (no height); `Resolves` feeds the
vertical axis.

### `fdoa` : [`FdoaCapability`](#fdoacapability)

FDOA velocity-measurement capability (DC-1055 #2). `Capable` for ESM-class
RWRs; `None` otherwise.

## `WarheadDef`

Warhead component parameters.

Direct-hit only -- damage is applied to the targeted entity with
distance-based falloff within `blast_radius`. No splash to other entities.

### `damage` : f32

Peak damage in hit points, dealt at the detonation point. Applied to the
single targeted entity and scaled down linearly with distance across
`blast_radius` (direct-hit model — no splash to other entities).

### `blast_radius` : f32

Lethal radius in metres: damage falls off linearly from `damage` at the
centre to zero at this distance; a target beyond it takes no damage.
Distinct from `proximity_fuze_radius_m`.

### `proximity_fuze_radius_m` : f32

True-proximity terminal-fuze trigger radius (m) for a range-blind PN weapon (#1696) — distinct from the lethal `blast_radius`.

## `SensorKind`

Kind of sensor hardware.

Variants:

- **`Radar`** — Active electromagnetic radar — emits and measures range + bearing.
- **`Ir`** — Passive infrared / thermal imager — bearing-only, reads heat signatures.
- **`Visual`** — Passive electro-optical (daylight camera) — bearing-only.
- **`Rwr`** — Radar-warning receiver / ESM — passive EM, detects and bears on emitters.
- **`SonarPassive`** — Passive sonar — listens for a platform's acoustic signature, bearing-only.
- **`SonarActive`** — Active sonar — pings and measures range + bearing underwater.

## `SensorCapability`

What a sensor can do, plus the capacity each capability carries.

Variants:

- **`Search`** — Search only — builds a track file by scanning; cannot lock.
  - `tracks` : [`ContactCapacity`](#contactcapacity) — Maximum number of contacts the search scan can hold in its track file.
  - `lock_detection` : bool — Whether the sensor warns when it is itself being fire-control-locked (the RWR / ESM / passive-sonar "being-locked" alert).
- **`Track`** — Fire-control only — cannot search; locks externally-cued tracks.
  - `locks` : [`LockCapacity`](#lockcapacity) — Maximum number of simultaneous fire-control locks.
- **`SearchAndTrack`** — Both — `concurrency` distinguishes AESA from mechanically-scanned.
  - `tracks` : [`ContactCapacity`](#contactcapacity) — Maximum number of contacts the search scan can hold in its track file.
  - `locks` : [`LockCapacity`](#lockcapacity) — Maximum number of simultaneous fire-control locks.
  - `concurrency` : [`TrackConcurrency`](#trackconcurrency) — Whether searching and locking happen at once (track-while-scan) or a lock suspends the search (mechanically-scanned).
  - `lock_detection` : bool — Whether the sensor warns when it is itself being fire-control-locked.

## `MountingPosition`

Where a sensor is physically mounted, determining Y-offset for horizon calculations.

Variants:

- **`Hull`** — Both detector and emitter at hull level (no mast offset).
- **`MastDetector`** — Detector at mast height, emitter at hull level.
- **`MastEmitter`** — Emitter at mast height, detector at hull level.
- **`MastBoth`** — Both detector and emitter at mast height.

## `SensorCharacteristics`

Sensor-kind-specific detection characteristics.

Replaces the per-field Options that were implicitly discriminated by `SensorKind`.
Radar and active sonar have doppler processing (notching); radar also has
look-down and low-altitude clutter models.

Variants:

- **`Radar`** — Radar: doppler notching + look-down clutter + low-altitude clutter.
  - `v_notch` : f32 — Max radial speed (m/s) below which target enters the notch.
  - `notch_fraction` : f32 — Effective RCS fraction at zero radial velocity (0.0-1.0).
  - `look_down_clutter_max_db` : f32 — Max look-down clutter attenuation in dB.
  - `look_down_clutter_max_angle` : f32 — Depression angle (degrees below horizon) at which max look-down clutter is reached.
  - `low_altitude_clutter_max_db` : f32 — Max low-altitude clutter attenuation in dB for low-altitude targets.
  - `low_altitude_clutter_ceiling` : f32 — Altitude (metres) above which low-altitude clutter has no effect.
- **`SonarActive`** — Active sonar: doppler notching only.
  - `v_notch` : f32 — Max radial speed (m/s) below which target enters the notch.
  - `notch_fraction` : f32 — Effective RCS fraction at zero radial velocity (0.0-1.0).
- **`Simple`** — No kind-specific characteristics (passive sonar, IR, visual, RWR).

## `SensorPhysics`

Kind-specific physical parameters. The enum makes invalid combinations
unrepresentable (a passive sensor cannot carry a bandwidth; an optic cannot
carry a carrier frequency).

Variants:

- **`ActiveRadar`** — Active EM (radar): measures range + bearing.
  - `bandwidth_hz` : f32 — Transmitted signal bandwidth (Hz). Sets the range-resolution cell, `c / (2 · bandwidth)` — wider bandwidth resolves range more finely.
  - `carrier_freq_hz` : f32 — Carrier (centre) frequency (Hz). Sets the EM wavelength `c / carrier`, which with the aperture fixes the beamwidth (bearing accuracy).
  - `aperture_m` : f32 — Antenna aperture diameter (metres). Beamwidth is `wavelength / aperture`, so a larger dish gives a narrower beam and finer bearing.
- **`PassiveEm`** — Passive EM (RWR/ESM): bearing only, no range gate.
  - `carrier_freq_hz` : f32 — Carrier (centre) frequency (Hz) the receiver is tuned to. Sets the EM wavelength `c / carrier` used for the beamwidth.
  - `aperture_m` : f32 — Antenna aperture diameter (metres). Beamwidth is `wavelength / aperture` — the only accuracy lever for a bearing-only EM sensor.
- **`ActiveSonar`** — Active sonar: measures range + bearing (sound-speed in water).
  - `bandwidth_hz` : f32 — Transmitted pulse bandwidth (Hz). Sets the range-resolution cell, `sound_speed / (2 · bandwidth)`.
  - `frequency_hz` : f32 — Acoustic operating frequency (Hz). Wavelength is `sound_speed / frequency`, which with the array length fixes the beamwidth.
  - `array_length_m` : f32 — Hydrophone array length (metres). Angular resolution is `wavelength / array_length` — a longer array gives a narrower beam.
  - `sound_speed_mps` : f32 — Speed of sound in water (m/s), used to convert frequency to wavelength and bandwidth to a range cell.
- **`PassiveSonar`** — Passive sonar: bearing only, no range gate.
  - `frequency_hz` : f32 — Acoustic frequency (Hz) the array listens at. Wavelength is `sound_speed / frequency`, used for the beamwidth.
  - `array_length_m` : f32 — Hydrophone array length (metres). Angular resolution is `wavelength / array_length`.
  - `sound_speed_mps` : f32 — Speed of sound in water (m/s), used to convert frequency to wavelength.
- **`Optical`** — Passive optic (IR / Visual): angular only, detector-limited (IFOV).
  - `ifov_microrad` : f32 — Instantaneous field of view of one detector element (microradians) — the sensor's angular resolution directly, no diffraction term.

## `AuthoredNoise`

Measurement-noise sigmas with no first-principles formula — kept authored.

### `velocity_sigma_mps` : f32

1σ noise on a velocity measurement (m/s), passed straight through to the
runtime `MeasurementNoise`.

### `altitude_sigma_m` : f32

1σ noise on an altitude / elevation-derived height measurement (metres).

### `fdoa_sigma_mps` : f32

1σ noise on a differential-Doppler (FDOA) velocity measurement (m/s), used
by ESM sensor pairs (see `crate::FdoaCapability`).

## `SignatureCapability`

Per-sensor signature-recognition capability (DC-36.6). A capable sensor reads
the platform signature only when its return clears `min_snr_db`; the absent
state is named (not a sentinel) per the project's type-discipline rules.

Variants:

- **`None`** — Basic search radar / sonar — no signature recognition; guesses from speed.
- **`Capable`** — Micro-Doppler radar / DEMON sonar — reads the signature when SNR clears the threshold (dB).
  - `min_snr_db` : f32 — Minimum return SNR (dB) at which the platform signature can be read; below this the sensor falls back to guessing class from speed.

## `ElevationCapability`

Whether — and how well — a sensor resolves a target's elevation.

Variants:

- **`None`** — Cannot resolve elevation (2D search radar, passive sonar horizontal bearing, basic RWR/ESM). Leaves altitude honestly uncertain.
- **`Resolves`** — The sensor resolves elevation; its angular precision is `MeasurementNoise::elevation_sigma_deg` (a passive elevation-angle σ, sibling to `bearing_sigma_deg`). For an ACTIVE height-finder the direct-altitude σ in `MeasurementNoise::altitude_sigma_m` is used instead.

## `FdoaCapability`

Whether a passive sensor can contribute a differential-Doppler (FDOA)
velocity measurement against an emitting target when paired with another
such sensor (DC-1055).

Variants:

- **`None`** — Basic RWR — no FDOA contribution.
- **`Capable`** — ESM-class RWR — can contribute an FDOA velocity measurement.

## `ContactCapacity`

Maximum number of contacts a sensor can simultaneously hold.

Variants:

- **`Limited`**(u32) — A finite track file of `n` contacts.
- **`Unlimited`** — No cap — every detectable contact is held (today's behaviour).

## `LockCapacity`

Max simultaneous fire-control locks a track-capable sensor can hold. Finite
and ≥1 — there is no "unlimited" fire control, and a sensor that cannot lock
carries no `LockCapacity` at all (see `SensorCapability`).

### `0` : `NonZeroU32`

Maximum number of simultaneous fire-control locks (≥1).

## `TrackConcurrency`

For a sensor that can both search and track, whether it does so at once.

Variants:

- **`WhileScanning`** — Track-while-scan — searches and holds locks concurrently (modern AESA).
- **`ScanOrTrack`** — Exclusive — holding any lock suspends the search (mechanically-scanned).
